title: [ipv6_addr.c:48] lichen_eui64_to_iid no bounds check on iid output buffer
description:
Contract: lichen_eui64_to_iid writes 8 bytes to iid parameter.
Violation: No runtime validation that iid points to at least 8 bytes. The memcpy (line 67) writes exactly 8 bytes. If caller passes smaller buffer, undefined behavior.
Impact: Same as other array decay issues - relies on caller discipline. All current callers use correctly-sized buffers (local arrays of size 8).
Mitigation: Language limitation. Document prominently or add explicit length parameter.
close_reason: Added SECURITY comment documenting the caller contract for buffer sizes. Both lichen_eui64_to_iid and lichen_pubkey_to_iid now have explicit documentation that buffer sizes are not bounds-checked at runtime due to C array decay, matching the pattern used in lichen_sha256. This is an accepted C language limitation with caller contract documentation.
